domain: Mountaineering / 8000ers — the 14 eight-thousander peaks (Everest, K2, Kangchenjunga, Lhotse, Makalu, Cho Oyu, Dhaulagiri I, Manaslu, Nanga Parbat, Annapurna I, Gasherbrum I, Broad Peak, Gasherbrum II, Shishapangma): elevations & coordinates, first ascents, summit/death statistics, and each peak's signature events.

sources.name: Wikidata
sources.license: CC0
sources.coverage: All 14 peaks as individual items (Everest Q513, K2 Q41217, etc.) linked from the 'eight-thousander' class item Q185552. Properties: elevation (P2044), coordinates (P625), first-ascent date, mountain range (P4552), country (P17), prominence (P2660), plus sitelinks to every-language Wikipedia. CC0 = single safest bulk backbone: import coords/elevations/first-ascent dates/cross-language names/QIDs as stable join keys with zero attribution burden.
sources.formats: SPARQL (query.wikidata.org), REST/JSON (Special:EntityData/Q513.json), full RDF dumps (.ttl/.nt), CSV export from query UI
sources.sample_records: Q513 Mount Everest: elevation 8848.86 m, coord 27.988°N 86.925°E, first ascent 1953-05-29. | Q41217 K2: 8611 m, 35.8825°N 76.5133°E, range Karakoram. | Q185552 'eight-thousander' enumerates all 14 members in one SPARQL query.

sources.name: Wikipedia — 'Eight-thousander' + per-peak articles + 'List of deaths on eight-thousanders'
sources.license: CC-BY-SA-4.0 (prose); infobox facts themselves not copyrightable
sources.coverage: Master comparison table (elevation, prominence, range, country, first-ascent year+party, total ascents, total deaths), a long-form article per peak and per disaster, plus a curated fatality registry. Deepest narrative source for signature events. Reuse prose under CC-BY-SA (attribute + share-alike); extracted facts (heights, dates, names, counts) are free to restate in your own schema.
sources.formats: HTML, MediaWiki API (JSON), full XML dumps, per-article wikitext
sources.sample_records: Table row Annapurna I: 8091 m, 1st ascent 1950 (Herzog/Lachenal), ~30% historic fatality rate. | Manaslu 8163 m, 1956 (Imanishi/Gyalzen Norbu). | Deaths list: 340+ deaths on Everest through 2024.

sources.name: The Himalayan Database (Elizabeth Hawley archive)
sources.license: proprietary but free-to-use custom licence; underlying facts not copyrightable
sources.coverage: Authoritative expedition-level archive for the NEPAL Himalaya (490 peaks, 11,500+ expeditions, 90,800+ members, 1905–2024). Covers 8 of the 14 that lie in/on Nepal: Everest, Kangchenjunga, Lhotse, Makalu, Cho Oyu, Dhaulagiri I, Manaslu, Annapurna I. Per expedition: dates, route, camps, O2 use, summit success/failure, deaths, and biographies incl. Sherpas. Does NOT cover Karakoram peaks (K2, Nanga Parbat, GI, GII, Broad Peak) nor Tibet-side Shishapangma. Free since 2017 but ships under its own terms — do NOT redistribute the raw file wholesale; extract facts and cite.
sources.formats: Downloadable full DB (Windows program + DBF data files), online search UI, supplementary PDFs ('Himalaya by the Numbers')
sources.sample_records: Everest 1953 Spring British expedition → summit success, Hillary & Tenzing. | Manaslu cumulative: 3,317 summits / 90 deaths (2024). | Everest cumulative: 12,884 summits / 340 deaths (Dec 2024).

sources.name: 8000ers.com (Eberhard Jurgalski / Elevation Equality)
sources.license: proprietary; facts not copyrightable
sources.coverage: Reference statistics site covering ALL 14 peaks — fills the Karakoram + Tibet gap the Himalayan Database leaves. Per-peak ascent chronologies and downloadable fatality lists, the authoritative 'summiters of all 14' verification list, and standardized elevation/coordinate data via Jurgalski's 'Elevation Equality'. Guinness-cited authority for 14x8000 claims and disputed/partial summits. No open licence — scrape facts and cite; go-to for the 6 non-Nepal peaks.
sources.formats: HTML pages + downloadable per-peak ascent/fatality files (via /cms/en/download.html)
sources.sample_records: K2 fatalities file (id=179), Broad Peak fatalities file (id=170). | Deadliest-mountain stats (1950–2012): Annapurna 31.9%, K2 26.5%, Nanga Parbat 20.3%, Dhaulagiri 15.4%, Kangchenjunga 14.1%. | Definitive verified 14x8000 completers list.

sources.name: Internet Archive — pre-1929 official Everest expedition books (US public domain)
sources.license: public-domain (published pre-1929)
sources.coverage: Full primary-source narratives of the founding Everest expeditions, now US public domain and freely republishable verbatim: 'Mount Everest: The Reconnaissance' (1921), 'The Assault on Mount Everest 1922', 'The Fight for Everest 1924' (Norton, incl. Mallory's letters, the Mallory & Irvine disappearance, Norton's 8,572 m no-O2 record). The highest-value PD long-form text set in the domain. NB later canonical books (Herzog 'Annapurna' 1951, Buhl, Messner, Krakauer 1997) remain in copyright — index as references only.

Confirms all 14 lie only in the Himalaya (Nepal/Tibet) and Karakoram (Pakistan).

key_entities.name: Mount Everest (Sagarmatha / Chomolungma)
key_entities.one_liner: The highest mountain on Earth at 8,848.86 m, on the Nepal–Tibet border, first climbed in 1953.
key_entities.notable_facts: Elevation 8,848.86 m (2020 China–Nepal joint remeasurement); coord ~27.988°N, 86.925°E; first ascent 29 May 1953 by Edmund Hillary & Tenzing Norgay (British expedition); ~12,884 summits and ~340 deaths through Dec 2024; Wikidata Q513.

key_entities.name: K2 (Chhogori)
key_entities.one_liner: The world's second-highest peak (8,611 m) and the hardest and second-deadliest of the 14, on the China–Pakistan Karakoram border.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 31 July 1954 by Lino Lacedelli & Achille Compagnoni (Italian expedition, Ardito Desio leader); ~26.5% historic fatality rate; first winter ascent only Jan 2021 (all-Nepali Sherpa team); site of the 2008 disaster (11 dead); Wikidata Q41217.

key_entities.name: Kangchenjunga
key_entities.one_liner: Third-highest peak (8,586 m) on the Nepal–India (Sikkim) border, first climbed in 1955.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 25 May 1955 by George Band & Joe Brown (British), who stopped just short of the summit out of respect for local sacred belief — a tradition many later climbers honored; among the more dangerous 8000ers; Wikidata Q40197.

key_entities.name: Lhotse
key_entities.one_liner: Fourth-highest (8,516 m), sharing the South Col and much of the route with Everest, first climbed in 1956.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 18 May 1956 by Fritz Luchsinger & Ernst Reiss (Swiss); the unclimbed Lhotse South Face is one of the great Himalayan problems; connected to Everest via the South Col; Wikidata Q42211.

key_entities.name: Makalu
key_entities.one_liner: Fifth-highest (8,485 m), an isolated four-sided pyramid on the Nepal–Tibet border, first climbed in 1955.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 15 May 1955 by Lionel Terray & Jean Couzy (French expedition led by Jean Franco), noted for putting the whole team on the summit over successive days; steep and technical; Wikidata Q46934.

key_entities.name: Cho Oyu
key_entities.one_liner: Sixth-highest (8,188 m) and considered the most accessible 8000er, on the Nepal–Tibet border, first climbed in 1954.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 19 October 1954 by Herbert Tichy, Joseph Jöchler & Pasang Dawa Lama (Austrian) with a small, lightweight team; the most-summited 8000er after Everest; popular first-8000er objective; Wikidata Q46158.

key_entities.name: Dhaulagiri I
key_entities.one_liner: Seventh-highest (8,167 m) in central Nepal, first climbed in 1960 and one of the last of the 14 to fall.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 13 May 1960 by a Swiss/Austrian expedition (incl. Kurt Diemberger, Peter Diener, Ernst Forrer, Albin Schelbert, Nyima Dorje, Nawang Dorje) — first 8000er ascent supported by a fixed-wing aircraft; name means 'White Mountain'; Wikidata Q206828.

key_entities.name: Manaslu
key_entities.one_liner: Eighth-highest (8,163 m) in Nepal's Gorkha region, first climbed by a Japanese team in 1956.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 9 May 1956 by Toshio Imanishi & Gyalzen Norbu (Japanese) — 'the Japanese mountain'; ~3,317 summits / ~90 deaths through 2024; avalanche-prone; increasingly a commercial trade-route peak; Wikidata Q160538.

key_entities.name: Nanga Parbat
key_entities.one_liner: Ninth-highest (8,126 m), the westernmost 8000er in Pakistan, nicknamed 'Killer Mountain', first climbed solo in 1953.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 3 July 1953 by Hermann Buhl (Austro-German), alone and without oxygen in a legendary final push; ~31 deaths in the 1930s German attempts; huge Rupal Face; first winter ascent 2016; Wikidata Q106463.

key_entities.name: Annapurna I
key_entities.one_liner: Tenth-highest (8,091 m) but the very first 8000er ever climbed (1950), and the deadliest by fatality rate (~30%).
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 3 June 1950 by Maurice Herzog & Louis Lachenal (French) — first 8000er summited and done without prior reconnaissance; both suffered severe frostbite; highest fatality-to-summit ratio of all 14; Wikidata Q16400.

key_entities.name: Gasherbrum I (Hidden Peak / K5)
key_entities.one_liner: Eleventh-highest (8,080 m) in the Pakistan Karakoram, first climbed by Americans in 1958.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 5 July 1958 by Pete Schoening & Andy Kauffman (American); later famous for Messner & Habeler's 1975 alpine-style, small-team, no-siege ascent that reshaped Himalayan climbing philosophy; Wikidata Q212333.

key_entities.name: Broad Peak (K3)
key_entities.one_liner: Twelfth-highest (8,051 m) in the Karakoram beside K2, first climbed in 1957.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 9 June 1957 by Hermann Buhl, Kurt Diemberger, Marcus Schmuck & Fritz Wintersteller (Austrian) — alpine-style without high-altitude porters or supplemental oxygen, a landmark lightweight ascent; Buhl died weeks later on nearby Chogolisa; Wikidata Q104489.

key_entities.name: Gasherbrum II (K4)
key_entities.one_liner: Thirteenth-highest (8,035 m) in the Karakoram, first climbed in 1956, generally the least deadly 8000er.
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 7 July 1956 by Fritz Moravec, Josef Larch & Hans Willenpart (Austrian); first winter ascent Feb 2011 (Simone Moro, Denis Urubko, Cory Richards) — the first winter ascent of any Karakoram 8000er; Wikidata Q214026.

key_entities.name: Shishapangma (Xixabangma / Gosainthan)
key_entities.one_liner: Lowest of the 14 (8,027 m) and the only one entirely inside Tibet, the last 8000er to be first-climbed (1964).
key_entities.notable_facts: First ascent 2 May 1964 by a Chinese expedition (Xu Jing and team) — completed the summiting of all 14; wholly within China, so long access-restricted; a central-vs-main-summit distinction has caused disputed 'true summit' claims; Wikidata Q590210.

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner
key_entities.one_liner: Italian alpinist who in 1986 became the first person to summit all 14 eight-thousanders, all without supplemental oxygen.
key_entities.notable_facts: First to climb all 14 (completed with Makalu & Lhotse in 1986); with Peter Habeler made the first oxygen-free ascent of Everest (1978); solo oxygen-free Everest 1980; pioneer of alpine-style Himalayan climbing; set the purist '14x8000 without O2' benchmark.

key_entities.name: 1996 Mount Everest disaster
key_entities.one_liner: A May 1996 storm that killed 8 climbers in one day near the summit, the defining cautionary tale of commercial high-altitude guiding.
key_entities.notable_facts: Guides Rob Hall and Scott Fischer among the dead; popularized by Jon Krakauer's 'Into Thin Air' (1997, in copyright); catalyzed debate over commercialization, client experience, and turnaround-time discipline on Everest.

key_entities.name: 2008 K2 disaster
key_entities.one_liner: An August 2008 sequence of serac collapses and accidents in the Bottleneck that killed 11 climbers, the deadliest single event on K2.
key_entities.notable_facts: Ice fall in the Bottleneck couloir severed fixed ropes high on the peak, stranding descending climbers; 11 dead across multiple national teams; a landmark case study in fixed-rope dependence and descent-phase fatality risk on the 8000ers.

key_entities.name: George Mallory & Andrew Irvine (1924)
key_entities.one_liner: Two British climbers who disappeared high on Everest on 8 June 1924, leaving open whether they reached the summit 29 years before Hillary and Tenzing.
key_entities.notable_facts: Part of the 1924 British expedition; Norton reached ~8,572 m without oxygen days earlier; Mallory's body found in 1999, Irvine's remains reportedly located 2024; documented in the public-domain 'The Fight for Everest 1924'.

notes:
Coverage strategy: no single source covers all 14 peaks at expedition granularity, so split the backbone. (1) Wikidata (CC0) as the safest bulk join layer for elevations, coordinates, first-ascent dates, names, and stable QID keys — import freely, no attribution burden. (2) Himalayan Database for deep expedition/summit/death records on the 8 NEPAL peaks (Everest, Kangchenjunga, Lhotse, Makalu, Cho Oyu, Dhaulagiri I, Manaslu, Annapurna I) — it does NOT cover the Karakoram peaks (K2, Nanga Parbat, GI, GII, Broad Peak, in Pakistan) or the Tibet side. (3) 8000ers.com (Jurgalski) fills that gap — authority for all 14, the 6 non-Nepal peaks especially, and for adjudicating disputed/partial summits. Pair these three for full-14 coverage.

Licence discipline — the fact/expression split is the key lever: elevations, coordinates, dates, ascender names, summit counts, and death tallies are FACTS and not copyrightable, so extract and restate them from any source (Himalayan Database and 8000ers.com are free-to-read but ship under proprietary/custom terms — do NOT redistribute their files wholesale; do extract facts and cite). Prose narratives (Wikipedia CC-BY-SA; and all modern books — Herzog 'Annapurna' 1951, Buhl, Messner, Krakauer 'Into Thin Air' 1997) are copyrighted EXPRESSION — reuse Wikipedia text under CC-BY-SA (attribute + share-alike), treat books as citations only, never reproduce.

Safe-to-reproduce-verbatim public-domain layer: the pre-1929 official Everest expedition volumes on archive.org (1921 Reconnaissance, 1922 Assault, 1924 'The Fight for Everest') are US public domain — full text republishable, the richest PD long-form source in the domain. NASA Earth Observatory imagery is US-gov public domain — one of the very few sources whose PICTURES (not just facts) you can reuse; use for base maps. Almost all other peak/expedition photography is in copyright — link but mark not-directly-reusable.

Data caveats to encode in schema: (a) elevations have competing figures — Everest 8,848.86 m (2020 joint China–Nepal) vs older 8,848 m; store source+year per height. (b) 'true summit' disputes (notably Shishapangma central vs main, plus Annapurna/Manaslu/Dhaulagiri) mean summit counts differ between Himalayan Database and 8000ers.com — record source + disputed-flag, not a single number. (c) fatality rates are period-dependent (the cited Annapurna ~32% / K2 ~26.5% are Jurgalski's 1950–2012 window) — always store the measurement window. (d) commercialization sharply inflated Everest, Manaslu, and Cho Oyu summit counts — cumulative counts need an as-of date. First-ascent span is firm: 1950 (Annapurna, first) → 1964 (Shishapangma, last).

sources.coverage: >200,000 座山峰的海拔、突起度、孤立度、山脉层级、峰单(七大洲最高峰、各洲 list)与登山者记录。【无授权、依赖抓取——归为不可直接复用;用于交叉核验海拔/突起度事实,同类数据回落 Wikidata/GeoNames】
sources.formats: HTML(仅第三方社区抓取 CLI,无官方 API/dump)
sources.sample_records: Seven Summits 峰单及各峰 prominence;Aconcagua/Denali 突起度≈海拔

sources.name: Wikisource
sources.license: public-domain / CC-BY-SA(逐文标注)
sources.coverage: 经校对的公版登山文献全文(Whymper 等),结构化章节,便于按段落引用。【底本公版文本可复用;维基编者新增注释/导言可能为 CC-BY-SA】
sources.formats: HTML、MediaWiki API、dump
sources.sample_records: Whymper 作者页汇集《Scrambles》等公版著作校对文本

sources.name: HathiTrust Digital Library
sources.license: public-domain(1929 年前卷) / 受限(在版)
sources.coverage: 大量数字化登山史书刊(Alpine Journal、早期远征记、Mummery《My Climbs in the Alps and Caucasus》1895 等),公版卷可全文下载。【仅 public-domain 状态卷可整批复用,逐卷核版权】
sources.formats: 扫描 PDF、OCR txt(公版卷可整本下载)
sources.sample_records: Mummery 1895《My Climbs in the Alps and Caucasus》;早期 Appalachia/Alpine Journal 卷

key_entities.name: 马特洪峰 Matterhorn / Monte Cervino
key_entities.one_liner: 阿尔卑斯瑞士-意大利边界 4478 m 金字塔峰,登山黄金时代的象征,因 1865 首登事故成为登山史转折点。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1865-07-14 Edward Whymper 队首登;下撤时绳断,Croz、Hadow、Hudson、Douglas 四人坠亡;Wikidata Q1374;一手史料见 Whymper《Scrambles》(公版)。

key_entities.name: 艾格北壁 Eiger Nordwand
key_entities.one_liner: 伯尔尼阿尔卑斯 Eiger(3967 m)约 1800 m 北壁,20 世纪最具死亡名声的高山攀登舞台,人称'谋杀墙'(Mordwand)。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1938-07-24 首登北壁:Anderl Heckmair、Ludwig Vörg、Heinrich Harrer、Fritz Kasparek;此前 1936 Hinterstoisser 队全灭。

key_entities.name: Denali(原 Mount McKinley)
key_entities.one_liner: 北美最高峰,阿拉斯加 6190 m,以极端寒冷、大突起度与漫长冰川路线著称,是七大洲最高峰中技术/气候难度靠前的一座。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1913 首登:Hudson Stuck、Harry Karstens、Walter Harper(首个登顶者)、Robert Tatum;2015 官方复名 Denali;NPS 自 1979 发布年度登山统计(公版)。

key_entities.name: 阿空加瓜 Aconcagua
key_entities.one_liner: 南美暨西/南半球最高峰,阿根廷安第斯 6961 m,非技术'walk-up'但高反致死率不低。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1897 首登:瑞士向导 Matthias Zurbriggen(Edward FitzGerald 远征队);突起度≈海拔(6961 m),七大洲最高峰之一;Wikidata Q39739。

key_entities.name: 乞力马扎罗 Kilimanjaro / Uhuru Peak
key_entities.one_liner: 非洲最高峰,坦桑尼亚独立火山 5895 m,七大洲最高峰中最易抵达者,徒步可达顶但高反风险显著。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1889-10-06 首登 Kibo 峰顶:Hans Meyer 与 Ludwig Purtscheller;顶点 Uhuru Peak;冰川持续退缩的气候标志地。

key_entities.name: 七大洲最高峰 Seven Summits
key_entities.one_liner: 每大洲最高峰的集合登顶挑战,存在 Bass 与 Messner 两个版本之争(澳洲/大洋洲之别)。
key_entities.notable_facts: Bass 版用 Kosciuszko(2228 m),Messner 版用 Carstensz Pyramid/Puncak Jaya(4884 m);Richard Bass 1985 首个完成(Bass 版);Messner 提出更难的 Carstensz 版。

key_entities.name: 卡斯滕兹金字塔 Carstensz Pyramid / Puncak Jaya
key_entities.one_liner: 大洋洲最高峰(印尼新几内亚 4884 m),Messner 版七大洲最高峰,唯一需真正技术攀岩的一座。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1962 首登:Heinrich Harrer 队;赤道附近石灰岩壁,进山受政治/许可限制;与 Kosciuszko 之争的核心。

key_entities.name: 约塞米蒂大岩壁:El Capitan / The Nose
key_entities.one_liner: 约塞米蒂谷约 900 m 花岗岩巨壁,现代大岩壁(big wall)攀登圣地,The Nose 是世界最著名岩线。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1958 The Nose 首登:Warren Harding、Wayne Merry、George Whitmore(分段耗时约 47 天);2017 Alex Honnold Freerider 无保护 free solo。

key_entities.name: 半穹顶 Half Dome
key_entities.one_liner: 约塞米蒂标志性穹顶,西北壁与顶峰攀登史贯穿从 19 世纪拓荒到现代自由攀登。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1875 George Anderson 首登顶(钻孔布绳);1957 Royal Robbins 队首登西北壁(Regular NW Face);John Muir 称其最崇高的岩体(公版著作)。

key_entities.name: 黎明墙自由攀登 Dawn Wall Free Ascent
key_entities.one_liner: 2015 年 El Capitan 黎明墙首次完全自由攀登,被视为当代最难大岩壁自由攀登之一。
key_entities.notable_facts: 2015-01-14 Tommy Caldwell 与 Kevin Jorgeson 完成,历时 19 天;32 段绳距、多段 5.14 难度;全球主流媒体报道。

key_entities.name: Edward Whymper
key_entities.one_liner: 英国登山家、版画家,马特洪峰首登者,其著作是阿尔卑斯登山黄金时代的核心一手史料。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1840–1911(著作与插图已入公有领域);《Scrambles Amongst the Alps 1860-69》《The Ascent of the Matterhorn》均可全文复用。

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner
key_entities.one_liner: 史上最具影响力登山家之一,提出更严格的 Messner 版七大洲最高峰,重塑高山与阿尔卑斯式攀登理念。
key_entities.notable_facts: 倡导以 Carstensz 取代 Kosciuszko;虽以 8000m 无氧闻名,其登山哲学深刻影响本子领域的标志峰认知。

key_entities.name: Heinrich Harrer
key_entities.one_liner: 奥地利登山家,艾格北壁 1938 首登四人组之一,后以《西藏七年》闻名。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1938 艾格北壁首登成员;1962 卡斯滕兹金字塔首登成员——横跨本子领域两大标志性事件。

notes:
研究方法:Wikidata/Wikipedia/Gutenberg/Internet Archive/NPS/AAC 官方页交叉核验,已确认各源许可与整批下载能力。

可整批复用的结构化源(推荐做数据地基):1) Wikidata(CC0,SPARQL+RDF dump,按'七大洲最高峰'/'大岩壁'过滤即可导全量事实,零署名义务,首选);2) GeoNames(CC-BY,CSV dump,坐标/多语别名底表);3) OpenStreetMap(ODbL,Overpass/Planet dump,海拔与几何,注意 share-alike);4) NPS Denali 年度统计(美国联邦公版,Denali 登顶时间序列)。

确定进入公有领域的老文本(可整本采集全文+插图):Whymper《Scrambles/Ascent of the Matterhorn》(卒 1911)、John Muir 约塞米蒂著作(卒 1914)、Mummery《My Climbs》(1895)、Alpine Journal 1863–~1929 卷;经 Gutenberg/Wikisource/Internet Archive/HathiTrust 分发。

事实 vs 表达的边界:海拔/坐标/首登日期/登顶者名单等属事实,不受版权,可自由采集(即便来源是有版权站点如 Peakbagger/AAC);受版权的是文章文字表达、近人著作、现代照片。

不可直接复用(仅作事实核验/线索,勿抓正文):Peakbagger(无授权无 API)、AAC Publications(免费阅读但 © AAC)、Wikimedia Commons/Alpine Journal 1930 年后卷中的现代照片与在版内容(须逐件核许可)。

覆盖缺口/后续:1) 阿尔卑斯各国官方测绘(如 swisstopo)许可待核,可补权威海拔;2) 大岩壁路线库 Mountain Project 为 CC-BY-NC-SA,商用受限,未纳入,但可作路线拓扑补充;3) 人物生卒/照片版权状态需逐条核验以判可复用性。

domain: mountaineering / expeditions — chronological milestone expeditions & first ascents (1786 Mont Blanc → present): Golden Age of Alpinism, the 8000ers race (1950 Annapurna, 1953 Everest, 1954 K2), Everest North/South Col routes, and modern alpine-style milestones

sources.name: Wikidata
sources.license: CC0 (structured data is public-domain-equivalent; fully reusable, no attribution required)
sources.coverage: Best batch-downloadable, licence-clean structured backbone. Items for essentially every named peak, notable first ascent, expedition and mountaineer, with typed statements: elevation P2044, coordinates P625, country P17, mountain range P4552, part-of massif, 'significant event' P793 (with point-in-time P585 qualifier used for first ascents), 'is a list of' P360→'first ascent' Q1194369. Facts are cross-linked to Wikipedia, VIAF, GND. Coverage of exact first-ascent parties/dates is uneven and often lives in linked Wikipedia prose rather than a single property, so treat Wikidata as the spine and enrich from text sources.
sources.formats: SPARQL endpoint (query.wikidata.org), REST/JSON per-entity (wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/Q513.json), full RDF/JSON dumps (dumps.wikimedia.org)
sources.sample_records: Q513 Mount Everest — elevation 8848.86 m, coordinates 27.988°N 86.925°E, located in Nepal/China. Q1194369 'first ascent' — concept node. Q337438 'Notable first ascents (mountaineering)' — Wikimedia list, P360→Q1194369. Q10519 Matterhorn — elevation 4478 m, first-ascent event linked to 14 July 1865.

sources.name: Wikipedia (English + other language editions)
sources.license: CC-BY-SA 4.0 (text; attribution + share-alike required). NOTE: underlying facts — who summited what peak on which date — are NOT copyrightable and can be extracted freely; only the prose expression is licensed.
sources.coverage: Richest narrative source for the full 1786→present chronology: dedicated articles for nearly every milestone (First ascent of the Matterhorn, 1953 British Mount Everest expedition, 1950 French Annapurna expedition, 1954 Italian K2 expedition, Eight-thousander, Golden age of alpinism), plus curated list-articles that ARE the chronological archive skeleton. Infoboxes give structured first-ascent party, date, route.
sources.formats: HTML, MediaWiki API (action=query / REST), full XML dumps (dumps.wikimedia.org), wikitext
sources.sample_records: 'List of first ascents of mountain summits' — Mont Blanc, 8 Aug 1786, Jacques Balmat & Michel-Gabriel Paccard. 'First ascent of the Matterhorn' — 14 Jul 1865, Whymper party, 4 killed on descent. 'Eight-thousander' — Annapurna I 3 Jun 1950 first 8000er ever climbed.

sources.name: The Himalayan Database (Expedition Archives of Elizabeth Hawley)
sources.license: Proprietary but free-to-use — maintained by the non-profit The Himalayan Database (est. 2017), free download since 2017. No open CC licence; redistribution of the compiled database is restricted, BUT the underlying facts (expedition dates, members, summit/death outcomes) are not copyrightable and can be ingested. Treat as authoritative fact source, not as a redistributable dump.
sources.coverage: THE definitive structured record for Himalayan expeditions, 1905–Spring 2022, covering all significant Nepal peaks plus both sides of border peaks (Everest, Cho Oyu, Makalu, Kangchenjunga). ~471 peak records, 10,800+ expedition records, 81,900+ member records, 15,200+ literature references. Per-expedition: dates, routes, camps, supplemental-oxygen use, summit success, deaths/accidents. Directly answers 'who first climbed peak X and every expedition since.'
sources.formats: Downloadable Windows program (Himal 2.7) over a DBF/dBASE dataset; Excel export of query results; PDF guides & summary reports (HimalayaByNbrs.pdf). Community CSV extracts exist (see TidyTuesday below).
sources.sample_records: Everest first ascent expedition 1953 (British, Hillary/Tenzing, South Col route, O2 used). Annapurna I 1950 (French, Herzog/Lachenal). Peak record incl. height, first-ascent expedition ID, cumulative summit/death counts.

sources.name: TidyTuesday 'Himalayan Database' CSV extract (2025-01-21)
sources.license: Derived from Himalayan Database; the tidied CSVs are shared for educational use via the TidyTuesday project (GitHub). Same fact-vs-expression caveat as the source DB — usable as a ready-made CSV ingestion path.
sources.coverage: Analysis-ready CSV flattening of Himalayan Database peaks + expeditions (peak_id, first-ascent year/country/host, height, expeditions, members, deaths). Convenient batch-download alternative to the Windows DBF program.
sources.formats: CSV (raw on GitHub / githubusercontent)
sources.sample_records: peaks.csv: EVER, Everest, 8849 m, first ascent 1953, Nepal/China. exped.csv rows keyed by peak+year with success/oxygen/death flags.

sources.name: AAC Publications — American Alpine Journal & Accidents archive
sources.license: Proprietary (© American Alpine Club); free to read online, NOT openly licensed — article text/photos are not redistributable. Factual expedition data (peak, route, party, date, outcome) is extractable. Older AAJ volumes (pre-1930, e.g. 1929 Vol.1) are entering/​in public domain via scans on Internet Archive.
sources.coverage: 20,000+ full-text expedition and first-ascent reports, 1929–2024, fully searchable, with an AAJ Comprehensive Index (peaks/routes/climbers). Primary-source authority for Greater Ranges first ascents and new routes worldwide; complements Himalayan Database beyond Nepal (Karakoram, Alaska, Andes, Antarctica).
sources.formats: HTML full-text + search UI + index pages; scanned volumes on Internet Archive (see below)
sources.sample_records: AAJ report on 1963 American Everest Expedition (West Ridge, Hornbein/Unsoeld). AAJ 1954 K2 Italian expedition account. AAJ Comprehensive Index entries by peak.

sources.name: Project Gutenberg — public-domain mountaineering classics
sources.license: Public domain (US) — fully reusable including verbatim text and original illustrations; the small Gutenberg trademark/licence applies only to the packaged files, strip it and the underlying work is free.
sources.coverage: Primary-source narratives of the Golden Age of Alpinism and early Himalayan reconnaissance — the load-bearing public-domain corpus for pre-1930 milestones. Full-text of the actual expedition accounts, ideal for quoting and building an old-books shelf.
sources.formats: plain-text (.txt), HTML, EPUB, Kindle
sources.sample_records: #41234 Whymper, 'Scrambles Amongst the Alps 1860-69' (Matterhorn 1865). #38044 Whymper, 'The Ascent of the Matterhorn'. #39421 Howard-Bury, 'Mount Everest: The Reconnaissance, 1921'. #69128 G.I. Finch, 'The Making of a Mountaineer'. Tyndall, 'Hours of Exercise in the Alps'.

sources.name: Internet Archive (archive.org)
sources.license: Mixed — large body of pre-1930 mountaineering books & journal runs are public domain (fully reusable); post-1930 scans may be lending-only/in-copyright (read but not redistribute). Check each item's rights statement.
sources.coverage: Deep scan archive: early American Alpine Journal volumes (1929–1932 Vol.1 onward), Hooker's 'Himalayan Journals' (1854), Alpine Journal runs, historic expedition monographs, plus borrowable modern AAJ years. Best source for high-res scans and OCR full text of the old public-domain shelf.
sources.formats: PDF, full-text OCR (.txt), EPUB, DjVu, JPEG page scans; metadata API
sources.sample_records: 'The American Alpine Journal 1929-1932 Vol 1'. 'Himalayan Journals' by J.D. Hooker (1854). Whymper 'Scrambles' Gutenberg mirror.

sources.name: DBpedia
sources.license: CC-BY-SA 3.0 (attribution + share-alike; inherits Wikipedia licence)
sources.coverage: RDF-ified extraction of Wikipedia infoboxes — mountains with elevation, first-ascent date/party, ranges, coordinates as queryable triples. A structured, batch-queryable alternative view of the same facts, useful for cross-checking Wikidata gaps in first-ascent parties that live in infoboxes.
sources.formats: SPARQL endpoint, RDF dumps (Turtle/N-Triples), Linked-Data URIs
sources.sample_records: dbpedia.org/page/Mount_Everest — elevation, first ascenders Edmund Hillary & Tenzing Norgay, first ascent 1953. dbpedia.org/page/Matterhorn — first ascent 1865.

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons
sources.license: Mixed but reuse-friendly — historic photos/maps generally public domain (pre-1930 / expired), modern uploads CC-BY / CC-BY-SA / CC0. Each file states its licence; filter to PD + CC for a safely reusable image pool. Do NOT assume famous modern summit photos (e.g. the 1953 Tenzing-on-summit photo) are free — many remain in copyright.
sources.coverage: Reusable image/map layer for the archive: portraits of Whymper/Balmat, historic Alpine and Himalayan expedition photos, route maps, peak panoramas. Structured file metadata via Commons API / SDC (Structured Data on Commons, CC0).
sources.formats: JPEG/PNG/SVG/TIFF, MediaWiki + Commons API, SDC statements (JSON)
sources.sample_records: PD engravings from Whymper's 'Scrambles'. Historic photos of the 1924 & 1953 Everest expeditions (rights vary). Route map SVGs for Everest South Col / North Col.

sources.name: HIMALAYA journal + Digital Himalaya + EBHR
sources.license: HIMALAYA: CC-BY-NC-ND 4.0 (free to read/share, NON-commercial, NO derivatives — so NOT freely reusable in a commercial archive, cite only). EBHR: CC-BY 4.0 (fully reusable with attribution). Digital Himalaya scans: mixed/permission-based.
sources.coverage: Scholarly + regional context around Himalayan mountaineering history, Sherpa/porter history, expedition ethnography; secondary source for verification and context rather than a bulk fact dump. EBHR content is the openly-licensed subset.
sources.formats: PDF, HTML (OpenEdition / Edinburgh Diamond / Digital Commons platforms)
sources.sample_records: HIMALAYA back-issues on Sherpa mountaineering labour. EBHR (CC-BY) articles on Himalayan exploration history.

sources.name: OpenStreetMap
sources.license: ODbL 1.0 (Open Database License — free to use/redistribute with attribution + share-alike on the database)
sources.coverage: Authoritative open geospatial layer: peak nodes (natural=peak) with names, elevation (ele), and increasingly wikidata= links for cross-referencing. Supplies clean coordinates/geometry for mapping the chronology; complements Wikidata's WikiProject Mountains linkage effort.
sources.formats: Overpass API (JSON/XML), planet dumps (.osm.pbf), Nominatim geocoding
sources.sample_records: node natural=peak name=Mount Everest ele=8849 wikidata=Q513. node natural=peak name=K2 ele=8611. natural=peak Annapurna I ele=8091.

key_entities.name: Mont Blanc — first ascent (1786)
key_entities.one_liner: The founding event of mountaineering: the first ascent of the Alps' highest peak, taken as the chronological start (1786) of the whole discipline.
key_entities.notable_facts: 8 Aug 1786; Jacques Balmat (crystal hunter) & Dr Michel-Gabriel Paccard reached 4808 m; spurred by Horace-Bénédict de Saussure's prize offer; Saussure himself summited 1787. Marks the pre-history before the organized Golden Age.

key_entities.name: Golden Age of Alpinism (1854–1865)
key_entities.one_liner: The decade in which most major Alpine 4000 m peaks saw their first ascents, largely by British climbers with Swiss/Italian guides, bracketed by Wetterhorn (1854) and the Matterhorn (1865).
key_entities.notable_facts: Alfred Wills' Wetterhorn ascent (1854) conventionally opens it; the Matterhorn tragedy (1865) closes it. Founding of the Alpine Club (London, 1857), the world's first mountaineering club. Key figures: Whymper, Leslie Stephen, John Tyndall.

key_entities.name: First ascent of the Matterhorn (1865)
key_entities.one_liner: The dramatic and deadly climax of the Golden Age — first ascent of the Matterhorn followed by a fatal descent that made mountaineering front-page news.
key_entities.notable_facts: 14 Jul 1865; led by Edward Whymper via the Hörnli ridge; 7 on the team, 4 (Hadow, Hudson, Douglas, guide Michel Croz) killed when a rope broke on descent. Documented in Whymper's public-domain 'Scrambles Amongst the Alps'. Italian party (Carrel) reached the summit days later via the Italian ridge.

key_entities.name: Aconcagua — first ascent (1897)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of the highest peak outside Asia (6961 m) and highest in the Americas, extending milestone climbing to the Andes.
key_entities.notable_facts: 14 Jan 1897; Swiss guide Matthias Zurbriggen (solo to summit) on the FitzGerald expedition. Now the most-climbed of the Seven Summits' non-Everest peaks.

key_entities.name: Denali (Mt McKinley) — first ascent (1913)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of North America's highest peak (6190 m), a milestone in high-latitude cold-weather mountaineering.
key_entities.notable_facts: 7 Jun 1913; Hudson Stuck, Harry Karstens, Walter Harper (first to summit) & Robert Tatum. Followed the discredited 1906 false-summit claim of Frederick Cook.

key_entities.name: Early Everest expeditions & the Mallory–Irvine mystery (1921–1924)
key_entities.one_liner: The British reconnaissance and attempts on Everest's North (Tibetan) side that opened the world's highest peak and produced mountaineering's most enduring mystery.
key_entities.notable_facts: 1921 Reconnaissance (Howard-Bury; Mallory scouts North Col route — book is public domain). 1922 first use of bottled oxygen (Finch/Bruce reach ~8320 m). 8 Jun 1924 George Mallory & Andrew Irvine disappear high on the NE ridge — 'because it's there.' Mallory's body found 1999.

key_entities.name: Eiger North Face — first ascent (1938)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of the notorious 1800 m Eiger Nordwand ('Mordwand'), the emblematic hard Alpine north-face milestone.
key_entities.notable_facts: 21–24 Jul 1938; Austro-German rope of Heckmair, Vörg, Harrer, Kasparek. Followed years of fatal attempts (the 1936 Kurz tragedy). Defines the '6 great north faces of the Alps' era.

key_entities.name: Annapurna I — first ascent (1950)
key_entities.one_liner: The first eight-thousander ever climbed and the highest summit reached by humans at the time — the opening shot of the Himalayan Golden Age.
key_entities.notable_facts: 3 Jun 1950; French expedition, Maurice Herzog & Louis Lachenal to the 8091 m summit with no supplemental oxygen; catastrophic frostbite (amputations) on descent. Herzog's book 'Annapurna' became the best-selling mountaineering book ever. Annapurna remains the deadliest 8000er by summit-to-fatality ratio.

key_entities.name: Mount Everest — first ascent (1953)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of the highest point on Earth (8848.86 m), the single most famous mountaineering milestone.
key_entities.notable_facts: 29 May 1953, ~11:30; Edmund Hillary (NZ) & Tenzing Norgay (Sherpa) via the South Col / SE ridge (the now-standard route), British expedition led by John Hunt; bottled oxygen used. News reached London on Coronation Day (2 Jun 1953). The South Col route contrasts with the Tibetan North Col route of the 1920s attempts.

key_entities.name: Nanga Parbat — first ascent (1953)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of the 8126 m 'Killer Mountain,' achieved solo on the final push in one of mountaineering's greatest feats of endurance.
key_entities.notable_facts: 3 Jul 1953; Hermann Buhl (Austrian, German-Austrian expedition) soloed the last ~1300 m, without oxygen, surviving a night standing on a ledge. Ended a deadly German obsession (31 deaths in prior attempts, incl. 1934 & 1937 disasters).

key_entities.name: K2 — first ascent (1954)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of the world's second-highest (8611 m) and hardest of the giant peaks, the 'Savage Mountain.'
key_entities.notable_facts: 31 Jul 1954; Italian expedition led by Ardito Desio, summit by Lino Lacedelli & Achille Compagnoni via the Abruzzi Spur. Enduring controversy over Walter Bonatti and Hunza porter Mahdi's high bivouac supporting the oxygen supply. K2 has never been summited in winter until Jan 2021 (Nirmal Purja's all-Nepali/Sherpa team).

key_entities.name: The 8000-metre-peaks race (1950–1964)
key_entities.one_liner: The 14-year sprint in which all fourteen 8000 m peaks received their first ascents, the Himalayan analogue of the Alpine Golden Age.
key_entities.notable_facts: Annapurna 1950 → Shishapangma 1964 (last, Chinese team). Milestones between: Everest 1953, Nanga Parbat 1953, K2 1954, Cho Oyu 1954, Makalu 1955, Kangchenjunga 1955 (Band & Brown, stopped just short of the sacred summit), Lhotse & Manaslu & Gasherbrum II 1956, Broad Peak 1957, Gasherbrum I & Dhaulagiri 1960.

key_entities.name: Everest without supplemental oxygen (1978)
key_entities.one_liner: First ascent of Everest without bottled oxygen, shattering the belief that the 'death zone' summit was physiologically impossible to reach on lung power alone.
key_entities.notable_facts: 8 May 1978; Reinhold Messner & Peter Habeler. Messner followed with the first solo, oxygen-less Everest ascent (North side, monsoon, Aug 1980).

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner — all 14 eight-thousanders (completed 1986)
key_entities.one_liner: The South Tyrolean who redefined high-altitude mountaineering and became the first person to climb all fourteen 8000 m peaks, all without supplemental oxygen.
key_entities.notable_facts: Completed the 14 with Lhotse in Oct 1986 (started Nanga Parbat 1970, where brother Günther died). Pioneer of alpine-style on the giants; also first Everest without O2 (1978) and solo (1980). Jerzy Kukuczka became the 2nd to complete the 14 (1987), far faster.

key_entities.name: Elizabeth Hawley — chronicler of the Himalaya
key_entities.one_liner: The Kathmandu-based American journalist whose meticulous expedition interviews became the Himalayan Database, the authoritative record for the whole era.
key_entities.notable_facts: Documented Nepal Himalayan expeditions from 1963 until her death in 2018; her verification of summit claims was so trusted that ascents were 'unofficially confirmed by Miss Hawley.' Peak Peak 'Hawley' / Peak Hawley named for her. Her archive → the free Himalayan Database (with Richard Salisbury digitising).

key_entities.name: Everest South Col vs North Col routes
key_entities.one_liner: The two classic 'normal' routes on Everest — the Nepalese South Col route of the 1953 first ascent and the Tibetan North Col/NE-ridge route of the 1920s pioneers.
key_entities.notable_facts: South Col route: via Khumbu Icefall → Western Cwm → Lhotse Face → South Col (7900 m) → South Summit → Hillary Step → summit (first climbed 1953). North Col route: via East Rongbuk Glacier → North Col (7000 m) → NE ridge (attempted 1921–24, first climbed by a Chinese team 25 May 1960). These two 'col' routes remain the dominant commercial lines today.

notes:
RESEARCH CONFIDENCE & GAPS.

Batch-downloadable structured sources (the archive's data foundation): (1) Wikidata via SPARQL/JSON/RDF dumps — the ONLY fully licence-clean (CC0) structured spine, but its first-ascent party/date coverage is patchy and often lives only in linked prose. (2) The Himalayan Database — the single most authoritative structured expedition record (10,800+ expeditions, 1905–2022), free to download, but distributed as a Windows/DBF program with proprietary terms; ingest the FACTS, don't redistribute the compiled DB. A ready-made CSV path exists via the TidyTuesday 2025-01-21 extract on GitHub. (3) DBpedia and (4) OpenStreetMap/Overpass give additional batch-queryable structured layers (CC-BY-SA and ODbL respectively).

Confirmed public-domain old-text shelf (safe to reuse verbatim, incl. period illustrations): Project Gutenberg (Whymper's 'Scrambles Amongst the Alps' #41234 and 'Ascent of the Matterhorn' #38044; Howard-Bury 'Everest Reconnaissance 1921' #39421; Finch 'Making of a Mountaineer' #69128; Tyndall) and Internet Archive (early AAJ 1929–32, Hooker's 'Himalayan Journals' 1854, historic Alpine Journal runs). These cover roughly 1786→1930 primary narratives.

FACT vs EXPRESSION — the load-bearing distinction for this archive: summit dates, parties, routes, elevations and outcomes are facts, NOT copyrightable, so they can be freely ingested from ANY source including the Himalayan Database, AAJ, and current books. What is protected is the specific prose, photographs, and maps. Practically: build the chronological fact database from Himalayan Database + Wikidata + AAJ; pull reusable narrative/quotes and images ONLY from public-domain (Gutenberg/IA/PD Commons) and openly-licensed (CC0/CC-BY) sources.

NOT-DIRECTLY-REUSABLE (cite/verify only): AAJ article text & photos (© AAC, free to read), modern expedition books (Herzog's 'Annapurna', Buhl, Bonatti, Messner — in copyright), most famous 20th-c. summit photographs (e.g. the 1953 Tenzing summit photo — assume in copyright), and HIMALAYA journal (CC-BY-NC-ND: non-commercial + no-derivatives, so unusable in a commercial archive beyond citation). Wikipedia text is CC-BY-SA (reusable WITH attribution + share-alike) — cleanest to extract facts from it rather than copy prose.

VERIFICATION STATUS: source licences/coverage were checked against primary pages (himalayandatabase.com, wikidata.org, publications.americanalpineclub.org, gutenberg.org) this session. Wikidata's exact property usage for first ascents was only partially pinned down — mountains reliably carry elevation (P2044)/coordinates (P625) but first-ascent parties are frequently modelled via 'significant event' (P793) qualifiers or absent, so a schema step should plan to enrich first-ascent fields from Himalayan Database + Wikipedia infoboxes rather than expecting a single clean Wikidata property. Key_entities facts are high-confidence standard mountaineering history; exact dates/times are as generally recorded but a couple (e.g. precise K2 Bonatti-affair details) remain historically contested and should be flagged in the archive."

key_entities.name: Edmund Hillary
key_entities.one_liner: 新西兰养蜂人出身的登山家，与丹增·诺盖于 1953-05-29 完成人类首次登顶珠穆朗玛峰。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1919–2008；1953 英国珠峰远征队（John Hunt 领队）成员；后半生投身夏尔巴学校与医院建设（喜马拉雅信托）。Wikidata/Himalayan Database 均有权威条目。

key_entities.name: Tenzing Norgay
key_entities.one_liner: 尼泊尔/印度夏尔巴登山家，与 Hillary 并肩首登珠峰，象征夏尔巴对喜马拉雅登山的核心贡献。
key_entities.notable_facts: 约 1914–1986；1953 前已多次参与珠峰远征积累经验；两人始终不公开谁先踏顶以示平等。事实无版权，早期照片多已公版。

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner
key_entities.one_liner: 意大利/南蒂罗尔登山家，人类首位登顶全部14座8000米山峰、且 1980 首次无氧单人登顶珠峰。
key_entities.notable_facts: b.1944；1978 与 Peter Habeler 首次无氧登顶珠峰；1970 南迦帕尔巴特之行痛失弟弟 Günther。Wikidata Q189307；8000ers.com 列为'14座'首位完成者(1986)。

key_entities.name: Chris Bonington
key_entities.one_liner: 英国最具影响力的远征领队与登山家，主导 1970 安纳普尔纳南壁、1975 珠峰西南壁等里程碑式大墙远征。
key_entities.notable_facts: b.1934；擅长'围攻式'大型远征的组织；AAC Publications 与 Alpine Journal 有大量其远征一手报道，事实可交叉验证。

key_entities.name: Wanda Rutkiewicz
key_entities.one_liner: 波兰登山家，1986 成为首位登顶 K2 的女性、亦是首批登顶珠峰的欧洲女性之一。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1943–1992；1978 登顶珠峰；1992 在干城章嘉冲顶途中失踪遇难；波兰喜马拉雅黄金一代旗帜。Wikidata Q237493。

key_entities.name: Jerzy Kukuczka
key_entities.one_liner: 波兰登山家，全球第二位完成全部14座8000米山峰者，且几乎全靠新路线或冬季首攀完成，风格远比 Messner 艰险。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1948–1989；1987 完成 14 座（用时不到 8 年）；1989 于洛子峰南壁坠亡；'波兰冬季登山'时代代表。8000ers.com 列为 14 座次位完成者。

key_entities.name: Ueli Steck
key_entities.one_liner: 瑞士'瑞士机器'速攀大师，以阿尔卑斯北壁极速独攀（艾格北壁等）与高海拔轻量化风格闻名。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1976–2017；多次刷新艾格/大乔拉斯北壁速度纪录；2017-04-30 在努子峰为珠峰-洛子连穿做适应时坠亡。Himalayan Database/维基百科有条目。

key_entities.name: Lynn Hill
key_entities.one_liner: 美国自由攀登传奇，1993 首次完成 El Capitan 'The Nose' 路线的自由攀登，1994 更以一日内完成，震动攀岩界。
key_entities.notable_facts: b.1961；名言'It goes, boys'；竞技攀岩与大墙自由攀登跨界标志人物，把女性攀登者推到技术前沿。

key_entities.name: Edward Whymper
key_entities.one_liner: 英国登山家/雕版画家，1865 首登马特洪峰，下撤时四名队员坠亡，成为阿尔卑斯黄金时代的悲剧性收官。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1840–1911；著《Scrambles Amongst the Alps》（公版, Gutenberg #41234）——早期传奇的公版一手叙事底稿，可全文入库。

key_entities.name: 珠穆朗玛峰首登 (1953)
key_entities.one_liner: 1953-05-29 Hillary 与 Tenzing 完成人类首次登顶地球最高峰，是 20 世纪登山史的分水岭事件。
key_entities.notable_facts: John Hunt 领队的英国远征队；Himalayan Database 有该远征逐条记录（队员/日期/用氧/路线），事实可自由复用。

key_entities.name: 珠峰首次无氧登顶 (1978)
key_entities.one_liner: 1978 Messner 与 Habeler 首次不借助补充氧气登顶珠峰，证明人类生理极限被低估。
key_entities.notable_facts: 颠覆当时'无氧不可能登顶 8848 米'的共识；Himalayan Database/8000ers.com 均有权威记录。

key_entities.name: K2 (乔戈里峰)
key_entities.one_liner: 海拔 8611 米的世界第二高峰，以极端难度与高死亡率著称，是检验顶尖登山家的试金石。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1954 意大利队首登；Rutkiewicz 1986 女性首登；Wikidata 有坐标/海拔/首登结构化字段，8000ers.com 有完整登顶编年。

notes:
调研方法：Wikidata/Wikipedia/Himalayan Database/AAJ/Alpine Journal/8000ers.com/Gutenberg 均经本轮网络搜索交叉核实（非纯记忆）。

复用优先级（给档案库建库）：
1) 事实地基批量入库——首选 Wikidata(CC0，无条件复用) + Himalayan Database(声明公共领域，尼泊尔远征逐条事实)。这两个是可整批下载的结构化源，覆盖'人物×远征×山峰×日期×用氧×死亡'的核心事实矩阵。
2) 叙事/史料——公版老文本可全文入库、切片、再分发：Project Gutenberg(Whymper 等经典) + Internet Archive/Alpine Journal 1863–1929 旧卷(美国境内公共领域)。
3) 交叉验证/权威核对（可引用、慎复用表达）——8000ers.com(© Jurgalski，表达受版权、事实可抽取) + AAC Publications 全文(近现代报告受版权，作事实核验与合理使用摘引)。
4) 配图——Wikimedia Commons，须逐图核对许可；老照片/PD 可用，现代摄影多 CC-BY-SA 需署名+SA，在世人物近照多受专有版权，归为不可直接复用。

关键法律区分（贯穿建库）：'事实'（谁于何日登顶哪座峰、生卒、死因）不受版权保护，可自由抽取重写；'受版权保护的表达'（他人叙述段落、统计表排布、现代摄影）不可照搬。CC-BY-SA 源（维基百科/DBpedia/部分 Commons 图）复用需署名+相同方式共享；优先以 CC0 的 Wikidata 承载事实层、SA 源补缺并合规署名。

待办/开放问题：
- Himalayan Database 底层表结构（程序内 DBF 类格式）转 CSV/JSON 的 ETL 需实测；社区镜像(Maven Analytics、GitHub tacookson/data)可作现成 CSV 起点但字段可能被裁剪，建库应回官方程序取全字段。
- 波兰喜马拉雅黄金一代（Rutkiewicz/Kukuczka/Wielicki 等）最详资料常在波兰语维基/文献，需波兰语源补充。
- 具体人物肖像的可复用性须逐图在 Commons 核许可，本报告未逐一清点。

sources.name: The Himalayan Database (Elizabeth Hawley 远征档案)
sources.license: 专有 (proprietary) — 自 2017 年起免费下载,但无开放许可;为编纂型数据库,事实可用,整库不可再分发/再授权。用前须注明来源,商用需谨慎核实条款。
sources.coverage: 权威中的权威:1905 年至今尼泊尔喜马拉雅 340+ 座主要山峰的全部远征与队员记录,可按山峰/攀登者/季节/国籍/死亡率/死因检索。1996 珠峰各队队员与死亡记录、南迦帕尔巴特(尼泊尔侧不覆盖,K2/南迦在巴基斯坦不在其范围——注意其地理边界仅限尼泊尔喜马拉雅)。
sources.formats: Visual FoxPro 9 可执行程序 (43 MB ZIP) + 可选 SQL 版;底层 DBF 表可导出。Windows 原生,Mac 需 CrossOver/Wine。含多份 PDF 分析报告。
sources.sample_records: 每条 expedition 记录含:peak code、year、season、host country、termination reason、summit success、逐 member 的登顶/死亡/死因(如 AMS、fall、avalanche、exposure)、是否用氧。1996 春季 Everest 有多支队伍逐人条目可比对 Krakauer/Boukreev 叙事。

sources.coverage: 14 座八千米峰的登顶/死亡统计与年表,与 Himalayan Database 并列为八千米峰两大权威。含 Fatalities tables、各峰死亡率、登顶争议核验(Bottleneck 等)。覆盖 K2、Nanga Parbat、Annapurna 的死亡统计(本领域除珠峰外的巴基斯坦侧空白由它补齐)。
sources.formats: HTML 表格(手工解析);无官方 API/dump

key_entities.name: Mount Everest (珠穆朗玛峰 / Sagarmatha / Chomolungma)
key_entities.one_liner: 世界最高峰(8,849 m),1996 与 2015 等多起最著名商业登山灾难的舞台。
key_entities.notable_facts: 中尼边界。1996 年 5 月 10-11 日东南山脊暴风雪致 8 死,催生 Krakauer《Into Thin Air》。Wikidata Q513。

key_entities.name: K2 (乔戈里峰)
key_entities.one_liner: 世界第二高峰(8,611 m),死亡率约四分之一,'野蛮之峰',2008 灾难现场。
key_entities.notable_facts: 巴基斯坦-中国 Karakoram。首登 1954(意大利)。2008-08-01 Bottleneck 冰塔崩塌致 11 死。Wikidata Q43512。

key_entities.name: Nanga Parbat (南迦帕尔巴特 / 裸山)
key_entities.one_liner: 世界第九高峰(8,126 m),20 世纪上半叶德国登山的'魔山',早期灾难连绵。
key_entities.notable_facts: 巴基斯坦 Gilgit-Baltistan。1895 Mummery 首尝殒命;1934 Merkl 远征 9 死;1937 雪崩 16 死(史上最惨单次);1953 Buhl 首登;1970 Messner 兄弟悲剧。

key_entities.name: Annapurna (安纳普尔纳峰)
key_entities.one_liner: 首座被登顶的八千米峰(1950),却是死亡率最高(约 32%)的八千米峰。
key_entities.notable_facts: 尼泊尔。1950 Herzog/Lachenal 法国队首登(严重冻伤)。统计学上最致命的八千米峰。Wikidata Q192749。

key_entities.name: Siula Grande
key_entities.one_liner: 秘鲁安第斯山(6,344 m),Joe Simpson 与 Simon Yates 生还史诗《Touching the Void》的发生地。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1985 年西壁首登后下撤中 Simpson 摔断腿、Yates 割绳,Simpson 独自爬出冰裂缝生还。

key_entities.name: 1996 Mount Everest disaster (1996 珠峰山难)
key_entities.one_liner: 1996 年 5 月 10-11 日商业登山队在下撤途中遭暴风雪,单次 8 人罹难。
key_entities.notable_facts: Adventure Consultants(Rob Hall)与 Mountain Madness(Scott Fischer)两队为主;越过安全折返时间是核心诱因。Wikidata Q1145128。

key_entities.name: 2008 K2 disaster (2008 K2 山难)
key_entities.one_liner: 2008 年 8 月 1 日 Bottleneck 上方冰塔连环崩塌,固定绳被毁,11 名登山者死亡——K2 史上最惨一日。
key_entities.notable_facts: 约 25 人趁天窗冲顶;Ger McDonnell 为救三名被困者遇难。多队伍协作失灵与超时冲顶叠加。Wikidata Q1362175。

key_entities.name: 1937 Nanga Parbat disaster
key_entities.one_liner: 1937 年营地遭夜间雪崩,7 名德国登山者与 9 名 Sherpa 共 16 人罹难,史上最惨重单次登山事故。
key_entities.notable_facts: 延续 1934 Merkl 远征灾难,使南迦帕尔巴特成为战前德国民族登山的悲情象征。

key_entities.name: Touching the Void 事件 (1985)
key_entities.one_liner: 登山伦理与生还意志的经典案例:'割绳'抉择后奇迹爬出的三天。
key_entities.notable_facts: Joe Simpson 1988 同名著作、2003 同名纪录片。'是否该割绳'成为登山伦理教学范本。

key_entities.name: Rob Hall
key_entities.one_liner: 新西兰向导、Adventure Consultants 创始人,1996 珠峰灾难中为守护客户 Doug Hansen 殒命南峰上方。
key_entities.notable_facts: 临终经卫星电话与妻子通话,成为该事件最广为人知的片段。

key_entities.name: Scott Fischer
key_entities.one_liner: 美国向导、Mountain Madness 创始人,1996 珠峰灾难中在东南山脊力竭死亡。
key_entities.notable_facts: 与 Rob Hall 商业竞争关系是事件背景之一。

key_entities.name: Jon Krakauer
key_entities.one_liner: 《户外》记者兼当事登山者,《Into Thin Air》(1997)作者,把 1996 灾难写成全球畅销书。
key_entities.notable_facts: 其叙事与 Anatoli Boukreev《The Climb》存在著名争议;两书均在版权期,仅事实可复用。

key_entities.name: Anatoli Boukreev
key_entities.one_liner: 哈萨克/俄罗斯高山向导,1996 珠峰灾难当夜无氧多次返回营地上方救回数名客户。
key_entities.notable_facts: 与 Krakauer 就无氧攀登与救援决策争论;著《The Climb》。1997 年死于安纳普尔纳雪崩。

key_entities.name: Ger McDonnell (Gerard McDonnell)
key_entities.one_liner: 首位登顶 K2 的爱尔兰人,2008 灾难中为解救三名被困登山者放弃自救而牺牲。
key_entities.notable_facts: 被视为山难利他主义典范;事迹见《Dictionary of Irish Biography》与《The Summit》纪录片。

key_entities.name: Hermann Buhl
key_entities.one_liner: 奥地利登山家,1953 年独自完成南迦帕尔巴特最后冲顶,首登该峰且无氧——八千米峰史上壮举。
key_entities.notable_facts: 冲顶往返约 40 小时、露天站立过夜。1957 年死于 Chogolisa 雪檐崩塌。

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner
key_entities.one_liner: 史上最伟大高海拔登山家,1970 年南迦帕尔巴特远征中弟弟 Günther 于下撤途中遇难。
key_entities.notable_facts: 首位无氧登顶珠峰、首位完成全部 14 座八千米峰。Günther 之死引发数十年争议。

key_entities.name: Joe Simpson 与 Simon Yates
key_entities.one_liner: 《Touching the Void》两位当事人:断腿爬出冰裂缝的生还者,与作出'割绳'抉择的搭档。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1985 Siula Grande 事件当事人;Simpson 后成畅销登山作家。

key_entities.name: Elizabeth Hawley
key_entities.one_liner: 驻加德满都数十年的传奇登山记录者,Himalayan Database 缔造者,喜马拉雅登顶的事实权威。
key_entities.notable_facts: 本领域最重要的一手数据编纂者;其数据库是核验尼泊尔侧远征/死亡的黄金标准。2018 年去世。

key_entities.name: A.F. Mummery
key_entities.one_liner: 英国先锋登山家,1895 年在南迦帕尔巴特首次尝试中失踪遇难,开启八千米峰灾难史。
key_entities.notable_facts: 著《My Climbs in the Alps and Caucasus》(1895,已入公有领域,可全文复用)。

notes:
许可与复用要点(区分'事实'与'受版权保护的表达/图片'):

1) 可整批下载/开放复用的结构化源:Wikidata(CC0,SPARQL+周 dump,首选事实地基)、维基百科/Wikisource(CC-BY-SA,API+XML dump,需署名+相同共享)、DBpedia(CC-BY-SA SPARQL)。这三者可安全构成档案库事实骨架。

2) 免费但非开放许可、只能'引用事实不能整库复制'的权威源:Himalayan Database(免费下载但为专有编纂数据库,FoxPro/SQL,注意其地理范围仅限尼泊尔喜马拉雅——K2 与 Nanga Parbat 在巴基斯坦侧不覆盖)、8000ers.com(免费浏览、专有、补齐八千米峰死亡统计尤其巴基斯坦侧)、AAC Publications AAJ/ANAC(受版权、免费在线,一手当事人报告价值极高)。这些用于事实核验与交叉印证,不可整篇入库。

3) 确定进入公有领域的老文本:本子领域灾难多为 1985-2008 的现代事件,其奠基名著(Into Thin Air 1997、The Climb 1997、Touching the Void 1988、No Way Down 2010、Buried in the Sky 2012)全部仍在版权期,不可直接复用——只能取其中的'事实'(日期、死者、路线、天气),表达与文本不可复制。真正 PD 的直接关联文本是南迦帕尔巴特灾难史起点 Mummery《My Climbs in the Alps and Caucasus》(1895,Gutenberg/Archive.org/Wikisource 均有,可全文复用),以及 Whymper/Tyndall 等阿尔卑斯公版经典(背景史)。老 Alpine Journal / Himalayan Journal 卷(pre-1929 US-PD)含 1930 年代南迦远征早期通讯,可在 Internet Archive 取全文。

4) 图片红线:1996 珠峰、2008 K2 现场照片几乎全部受版权保护,不在可复用范围;Wikimedia Commons 可复用素材以峰体地形照、路线图、公版地图为主,须逐张核对授权并署名。

5) 建库建议:以 Wikidata QID 作主键串联山峰/事件/人物,维基百科正文做叙事层,Himalayan Database + 8000ers 做死亡/远征事实核验层,AAJ 做一手证据引用层,Mummery 等 PD 文本做可全文收录的历史文献层。数据源在地理与年代上互补(Himalayan Database 尼泊尔侧 vs 8000ers 全八千米峰),需做去重与交叉校验。

未决/待深挖:Himalayan Database 的确切再分发条款未在下载页明示,商用前应邮件向其确认;八千米峰逐人死亡名单可从维基百科 List of deaths on eight-thousanders 解析为结构化行,但需与 8000ers/Himalayan Database 三方对账消歧(登顶争议、死因分类口径不一)。

key_entities.name: 马特洪峰 (Matterhorn / Cervino)
key_entities.one_liner: 海拔 4478 m 的阿尔卑斯标志性金字塔峰,其 1865 年首登既是黄金时代的巅峰也是终章。
key_entities.notable_facts: 位于瑞士 Zermatt 与意大利边界;Wikidata Q1090;1865-07-14 由 Whymper 一行首登;下撤断绳致 4 人坠亡。

key_entities.name: 1865 年马特洪峰首登与惨案 (event)
key_entities.one_liner: 7 人登顶、下撤时断绳致 4 人坠亡的登山史转折事件,既终结黄金时代又把登山推入公众视野。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1865-07-14;登顶 7 人:Whymper、Croz、老少 Taugwalder、Lord Francis Douglas、Charles Hudson、Douglas Hadow;Hadow 失足连坠 Croz/Hudson/Douglas,备用旧绳断裂;Whymper 与 Taugwalder 父子生还,后被控割绳但获判无罪;Douglas 遗体仅寻回一截衣袖。

key_entities.name: Edward Whymper
key_entities.one_liner: 英国插画家兼登山家,马特洪峰首登领队,其著作《Scrambles》塑造了登山文学的原型。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1840-1911;Wikidata Q313283;著《Scrambles Amongst the Alps》(1871,公有领域);7 次尝试后于 1865 首登马特洪峰;亲绘书中木刻版画(公版可复用)。

key_entities.name: Michel Croz
key_entities.one_liner: 夏慕尼顶级向导,黄金时代众多首登的技术核心,在马特洪峰惨案中首个坠亡。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1830-1865;法国 Chamonix 向导;参与 Aiguille Verte、Grand Combin 等首登;1865 马特洪峰事故遇难,葬于 Zermatt 教堂旁。

key_entities.name: Peter Taugwalder (父)
key_entities.one_liner: Zermatt 本地向导,马特洪峰首登断绳事故的两名生还者之一,长期背负割绳嫌疑。
key_entities.notable_facts: 约 1820-1888;瑞士 Zermatt 向导;与其子随 Whymper 登顶生还;调查证断裂的是备用旧绳、无割绳证据,获判无罪。

key_entities.name: Alfred Wills
key_entities.one_liner: 1854 年登顶 Wetterhorn,一般被定为登山黄金时代的开端标志。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1828-1912;英国法官兼登山家;1854 Wetterhorn 登顶被视为黄金时代起点;后任 Alpine Club 主席;著《Wanderings Among the High Alps》。

key_entities.name: Wetterhorn 首登 (event)
key_entities.one_liner: Alfred Wills 1854 年的这次登顶,被英语史学界定为登山黄金时代的开幕。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1854 年;伯尔尼阿尔卑斯;实为多次登顶之一,但因 Wills 的畅销记述而成为象征性起点。

key_entities.name: Alpine Club (London)
key_entities.one_liner: 1857 年创立于伦敦的世界第一个登山俱乐部,黄金时代的组织中枢与话语中心。
key_entities.notable_facts: 成立 1857;Wikidata Q391162;出版《Peaks, Passes and Glaciers》(1859)及《The Alpine Journal》(1863- );早期会员几乎垄断黄金时代首登。

key_entities.name: The Alpine Journal (publication)
key_entities.one_liner: Alpine Club 1863 年创刊的会刊,19世纪登山首登与事故最权威的连续一手记录。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1863 创刊,至今连续出版;副题 'a record of mountain adventure and scientific observation';1900 年前各卷已入公有领域,Archive/HathiTrust 可整本下载。

key_entities.name: 《Scrambles Amongst the Alps》(publication)
key_entities.one_liner: Whymper 1871 年出版的登山回忆录,登山文学的奠基之作,含马特洪峰惨案第一手叙述。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1871 出版,记 1860-69 九年攀登;公有领域(Gutenberg #41234、Wikisource);Whymper 亲绘版画;马特洪峰断绳一节为经典文本。

key_entities.name: John Tyndall
key_entities.one_liner: 爱尔兰物理学家兼登山家,Whymper 的马特洪峰竞争对手,1861 年首登 Weisshorn。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1820-1893;冰川科学先驱;1861 首登 Weisshorn(4506 m);多次争夺马特洪峰意大利侧;代表'科学登山'一脉。

key_entities.name: Leslie Stephen
key_entities.one_liner: Alpine Club 主席、多座峰首登者,以《The Playground of Europe》确立登山散文传统。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1832-1904;首登 Schreckhorn(1861)、Zinalrothorn(1864)等;著《The Playground of Europe》(1871,公版);Virginia Woolf 之父。

key_entities.name: 黄金时代首登群:Weisshorn/Dent Blanche/Zinalrothorn 等 (peak cluster)
key_entities.one_liner: 1854-1865 间被首登的一批 4000 m 级瓦莱/伯尔尼高峰,构成黄金时代的成就主体。
key_entities.notable_facts: 含 Weisshorn(1861,Tyndall)、Dent Blanche(1862)、Zinalrothorn(1864,Stephen)、Barre des Écrins(1864)、Aiguille Verte(1865);1865 一年阿尔卑斯约 65 次首登。

key_entities.name: Mont Blanc 首登 (Balmat & Paccard, 1786)
key_entities.one_liner: 1786 年 Jacques Balmat 与 Michel Paccard 登顶西阿尔卑斯最高峰,被视为现代登山运动的起源事件。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1786-08-08;缘于 de Saussure 悬赏;早黄金时代近 70 年,是'登山运动起源'叙事的锚点;Mont Blanc 4808 m。

key_entities.name: Horace-Bénédict de Saussure
key_entities.one_liner: 日内瓦博物学家,以悬赏催生 Mont Blanc 首登、次年亲登,常被称为登山运动的思想奠基人。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1740-1799;1760 悬赏首登 Mont Blanc;1787 亲率队登顶做科学观测;著《Voyages dans les Alpes》(公版)。

key_entities.name: Lucy Walker
key_entities.one_liner: 英国女登山家,1871 年成为首位登顶马特洪峰的女性,标志女性进入白银时代登山。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1836-1916;活跃于 1858-1879,完成 98 次远征;1871-07 首位登顶马特洪峰的女性,赶在对手 Meta Brevoort 之前。

notes:
优先复用建议(按安全度排序):(1) 结构化骨架用 Wikidata(CC0,SPARQL 可整批拉山峰/人物/事件),无义务复用首选;(2) 全文语料用 Project Gutenberg + Wikisource + Internet Archive 的 1900 年前公有领域文本(Whymper《Scrambles》/《Ascent of the Matterhorn》、Alpine Journal 早卷、Stephen《Playground of Europe》、Saussure《Voyages dans les Alpes》)——可整本下载做可检索档案;(3) 叙事字段可从英文维基百科抽'事实'(日期/海拔/人名不受版权)后重写,若照搬表达须 CC-BY-SA 署名+同权。

版权红线:(a) 现代照片即便在 Commons 也常为 CC-BY-SA,只用标 PD-old 的 19 世纪版画/肖像/古地图;(b) 近人著作(如 2015《1865: the Golden Age of Mountaineering》)、SummitPost/Explorersweb/History Today 等博客与媒体文章为专有版权,只可作事实交叉验证,不可整段复用;(c) Alpine Club 官网与 American Alpine Club 库中老文章多公版但混有近人版权文章,需逐篇核实。

数据模型提示:Wikidata 没有统一的'首登'属性,首登事件多以 P793(significant event)+ point-in-time 限定符表达且覆盖不全,建议以维基百科 'First ascent of X' / 'Golden age of alpinism' 条目做首登年表补全后回填结构化表。时代边界口径可采用英语史学通行说法:黄金时代 1854(Wetterhorn)–1865(Matterhorn),白银时代 1865–1882(Dent du Géant)。

待补(下一步):瑞士侧可查 SAC(Schweizer Alpen-Club,1863 创立)及其会刊 Die Alpen 的数字化与许可;e-periodica.ch(瑞士期刊数字化平台)可能含 SAC 年鉴公版卷;法语 Wikisource 的 Saussure 全文;Zermatt/Chamonix 地方档案与向导名录。这些能加厚瑞士/法国本地向导与机构维度。所有关键事实已用网络搜索交叉验证(Wikipedia 'First ascent of the Matterhorn'、Golden age of alpinism、Gutenberg 目录、Archive 卷目),置信度高。

domain: mountaineering / science-physiology — 高海拔生理与医学(缺氧·习服·死亡地带·HAPE/HACE·供氧史·人体极限)

sources.name: Internet Archive — Mosso《Life of Man on the High Alps》(1898)
sources.license: public-domain(1898 出版,版权已过期)
sources.coverage: Angelo Mosso 在 Monte Rosa Capanna Margherita 高山实验室的经典专著英译本,山地病、周期性呼吸(Cheyne-Stokes)、二氧化碳/缺氧之争的早期原始文献。全书可整批下载。
sources.formats: full-text TXT / PDF / EPUB / DjVu / OCR
sources.sample_records: 记述高山周期性呼吸观测;Mosso 提出『acapnia(低碳酸血症)』假说解释山地病(后被证伪,但为原始史料);含 Capanna Regina Margherita(4559m,世界最高常设实验室)建站记录。

sources.name: Internet Archive — Paul Bert《Barometric Pressure / La Pression barométrique》(1878)
sources.license: public-domain(1878 法文原著版权已过期;注意:1943 年 Hitchcock 英译本可能仍受版权,需单独核实,不直接复用)
sources.coverage: 高海拔生理学奠基之作。系统证明高山病的根本原因是低氧分压(而非低气压本身),奠定『缺氧=病因』范式。法文原著整批可下载;Wellcome Collection 另存节选。
sources.formats: full-text TXT / PDF / DjVu(法文);英译节选见 Wellcome Collection HTML
sources.sample_records: 实验证明减压舱内动物症状取决于氧分压;为后世 alveolar PO2、氧饱和度理论提供实验基础;被公认为『高原生理学之父』的核心著作。

sources.name: Project Gutenberg — 《The Assault on Mount Everest, 1922》(C. G. Bruce 等)
sources.license: public-domain(Gutenberg 已核定 US 公有领域)
sources.coverage: 1922 远征官方报告,含 George Ingle Finch 亲述首次系统使用瓶装氧(『English air』)、习服策略、8320m 登高记录。史实+一手叙述,可整批复用。
sources.formats: HTML / plain TXT / EPUB / Kindle
sources.sample_records: Finch 与 Geoffrey Bruce 登至 8320m(时人类最高),上升速率约 290 m/h;搬运工/藏尼向导戏称氧气瓶为『英国空气』;记述交替步行与骑马以促进习服。LibriVox 另有配套公有领域有声版(https://librivox.org/the-assault-on-mount-everest-1922-by-various-authors/)。

sources.name: PMC (PubMed Central, NIH) — 高原生理开放获取文献
sources.license: 混合(部分 CC-BY/CC-BY-NC 开放获取;多数仅免费阅读,版权归作者/期刊)。事实数据可复用,受版权保护的是表达文字。
sources.coverage: 含 John B. West《High Life: A History of High-Altitude Physiology and Medicine》书评/条目、Operation Everest II 系列论文、AMREE 1981 数据、Kellas/Finch 史学论文、2018 Lake Louise Score。权威二次文献,用于事实核对与年表锚定。
sources.formats: HTML / PDF /(开放获取子集提供 XML 批量:PMC Open Access Subset,可 FTP 整批下载 CC 授权文章)
sources.sample_records: West 实测 Everest 峰顶气压约 253 mmHg(远高于标准大气模型外推值,决定了无氧登顶在生理上勉强可能);Operation Everest II(1985,USARIEM)减压舱模拟登顶 40 天,VO2max 由 4.13 降至 1.17 L/min。

sources.name: Wilderness Medical Society Clinical Practice Guidelines(高原病防治指南)
sources.license: proprietary(Elsevier/SAGE 版权;临床事实与分级建议可作事实引用,原文不可复制)。多版有免费 PDF 流出(mountainguides.com 等)。
sources.coverage: AMS/HACE/HAPE 的预防、诊断、治疗循证指南(2010 原版 / 2019 / 2024 更新)。海拔阈值、上升速率建议、乙酰唑胺/地塞米松/硝苯地平方案、下撤原则等标准化事实。
sources.formats: PDF / HTML
sources.sample_records: 建议 3000m 以上每日睡眠海拔升幅 ≤500m、每升 1000m 增一休整日;HAPE 定义为高原非心源性肺水肿,一线处置=下撤+吸氧+硝苯地平;HACE=共济失调+意识改变,需即刻下撤+地塞米松。

sources.coverage: 临床权威科普:AMS/HAPE/HACE 定义、病理、Lake Louise 评分、预防用药、海拔分级(high 1500–3500 / very high 3500–5500 / extreme >5500m)。适合做条目事实校验基线。

sources.coverage: 官方托管最新 Lake Louise AMS 评分表、STAR 高原临床研究数据报告规范、会员机构与共识文件入口。用于标准化术语与评分锚点。
sources.formats: HTML / PDF
sources.sample_records: 2018 Lake Louise AMS Score(头痛+胃肠+乏力+头晕四项自评,阈值判定 AMS);STAR 报告指南统一高原研究的海拔/暴露时长/上升速率报告字段。

sources.name: Journal of Applied Physiology — West 高原生理史学系列 & AMREE 数据
sources.license: proprietary(American Physiological Society 版权;部分历史文自由获取)。事实/测量数据可引用。
sources.coverage: 供氧史与人体极限的权威一手/二手来源:Finch 1922 氧装置、Kellas 1920 手稿分析、Operation Everest II、1981 American Medical Research Expedition to Everest(AMREE)峰顶气体样本与气压实测。
sources.formats: HTML / PDF / abstract
sources.sample_records: 『George I. Finch and his pioneering use of oxygen』(2003);『Alexander M. Kellas and the physiological challenge of Mt. Everest』(1987);AMREE 采集峰顶肺泡气样,alveolar PO2 约 35 mmHg、PCO2 约 7.5 mmHg。

key_entities.name: 死亡地带(Death Zone)
key_entities.one_liner: 海拔约 8000m 以上、氧分压过低使人体无法习服、机能持续衰退的高度带。
key_entities.notable_facts: 术语由瑞士医生 Édouard Wyss-Dunant(1953)提出为『lethal zone』;峰顶气压约为海平面的 1/3;停留越久意识丧失与死亡风险越高;14 座 8000m 级山峰均部分处于此带。Wikidata Q1194687。

key_entities.name: 低压性缺氧与高原习服(Hypobaric Hypoxia & Acclimatization)
key_entities.one_liner: 海拔升高致气压下降、氧分压降低,人体通过通气、血液与循环系统代偿性适应的过程。
key_entities.notable_facts: 机制:过度换气(HVR)、红细胞生成素↑致红细胞增多、心率↑、2,3-DPG 调节、肺动脉压↑;需数天至数周;习服有上限,>8000m 只能耗竭。Paul Bert 1878 确立缺氧为病因。

key_entities.name: 急性高山病(Acute Mountain Sickness, AMS)
key_entities.one_liner: 快速上升至高海拔后出现头痛、恶心、乏力、头晕的最常见轻症高原反应。
key_entities.notable_facts: 常见于 >2500m;用 2018 Lake Louise Score 评分(头痛为必备项);可进展为 HACE;预防用乙酰唑胺、缓升、『爬高睡低』。

key_entities.name: 高原肺水肿(HAPE)
key_entities.one_liner: 高海拔非心源性肺水肿,缺氧性肺血管收缩不均致毛细血管渗漏,致命高原急症之一。
key_entities.notable_facts: ICD-10 T70.2;症状:休息时呼吸困难、咳粉红泡沫痰、发绀;一线处置=立即下撤+吸氧+硝苯地平;个体易感性差异大。Wikidata Q621636。

key_entities.name: 高原脑水肿(HACE)
key_entities.one_liner: AMS 的严重终末阶段,脑组织水肿致共济失调、意识障碍,数小时内可致死。
key_entities.notable_facts: 标志性体征:走直线共济失调、精神状态改变;处置=即刻下撤+地塞米松+吸氧+高压袋(Gamow Bag);常与 HAPE 并发。

key_entities.name: Everest 峰顶气压 ≈ 253 mmHg(人体极限锚点)
key_entities.one_liner: John B. West 领队的 1981 AMREE 实测珠峰顶气压约 253 Torr,揭示无氧登顶在生理上勉强可能。
key_entities.notable_facts: 实测值高于标准大气外推,因赤道附近平流层增厚;峰顶肺泡 PO2 仅约 35 mmHg;这一数据解释了 1978 年 Messner/Habeler 无氧登顶为何可行。

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner & Peter Habeler(1978 首次无氧登顶珠峰)
key_entities.one_liner: 1978 年 5 月两人首次不借助瓶装氧登顶珠峰,证明人体极限可及死亡地带之巅。
key_entities.notable_facts: 此前生理学界普遍认为无氧登顶不可能;Messner 1980 再完成无氧单人登顶;事件成为高原生理学『人体极限』研究的关键实证。

key_entities.name: George Ingle Finch(瓶装氧登山先驱)
key_entities.one_liner: 1922 珠峰远征首次系统证明补充供氧的价值,登至 8320m,其开路式(open-circuit)氧装置原理沿用至今。
key_entities.notable_facts: 物理化学家出身,亲自设计氧装置;与 Geoffrey Bruce 上升速率约 290 m/h;porters 称氧气瓶为『English air』;现代开路供氧系统可溯源至此。

key_entities.name: Alexander M. Kellas(高原生理理论奠基者)
key_entities.one_liner: 英国化学家/登山家,1920 年未刊手稿首次定量论证登顶珠峰的生理可行性。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1868–1921;八次喜马拉雅远征;推算峰顶气压、alveolar PO2、氧饱和度、最大摄氧量与登高速率;1921 远征途中病逝;手稿存皇家地理学会与 Alpine Club。

key_entities.name: Operation Everest I(1946)& II(1985)
key_entities.one_liner: 在减压舱内模拟渐进登顶珠峰的里程碑式人体生理实验,系统测量极端缺氧下的生理反应。
key_entities.notable_facts: OE I 由 Charles Houston 主持;OE II(1985,USARIEM)40 天减压至 240 Torr,VO2max 由 4.13 降至 1.17 L/min,测得肺动脉压/肺血管阻力升高但无右心衰。

key_entities.name: Paul Bert(高原生理学之父)
key_entities.one_liner: 1878《La Pression barométrique》以减压舱实验证明高山病病因是低氧分压而非低气压本身。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1833–1886;奠定缺氧病因范式与航空/高原医学基础;著作已入公有领域(法文原著);同时是气压性减压病研究先驱。

key_entities.name: Lake Louise Score(高山病标准化评分)
key_entities.one_liner: 1991 年首创、2018 年修订的 AMS 自评量表,是高原研究与临床沟通的通用术语基准。
key_entities.notable_facts: 四维度自评(头痛/胃肠/乏力/头晕),2018 版将睡眠障碍剔除;由 ISMM 世界大会与国际缺氧研讨会共识修订;为档案库统一『高原反应严重度』字段的锚点。

notes:
可安全整批复用的两类首选:①结构化/可 dump 源——Wikidata(CC0,SPARQL 一次拉全部『高原疾病』子类含 ICD/MeSH 编码,零版权风险)、Wikipedia dumps(CC-BY-SA,需署名同协议)、PMC Open Access Subset(CC 授权子集可 FTP 批量);②确定进入公有领域的老文本——Mosso《Life of Man on the High Alps》1898、Paul Bert《La Pression barométrique》1878 法文原著、Project Gutenberg《The Assault on Mount Everest, 1922》(附 LibriVox 公有领域有声版)。

版权红线提醒:(a)事实/测量数据(如珠峰顶 253 mmHg、VO2max 数值、海拔阈值)不受版权保护,可自由录入档案库;受保护的是具体表达文字与图片。(b)WMS 指南、MSD Manual、Journal of Applied Physiology、High Altitude Medicine & Biology 等均为专有版权,只能作事实来源与引用锚点,正文与图表不可复制。(c)Paul Bert 1943 年 Hitchcock 英译本、West《High Life》(2013)等近人著作仍受版权,归为『不可直接复用』,仅供事实核对。(d)近现代远征照片(如 1953 珠峰、Messner 影像)基本受版权保护,须逐张核实,默认不可复用。

覆盖缺口/后续:①尚未逐一核实 Wikidata 各高原病实体的 QID 完整性,建库时应实跑一次 SPARQL 导出并人工校对 ICD-11 映射。②供氧技术史(开路 vs 闭路、Draeger/Poisk/现代文丘里系统)有专门二次文献(Windsor & Rodway 系列),多为专有版权,可作年表事实源。③非欧裔高原原住民生理适应(藏族 EPAS1、安第斯 Monge 病/慢性高山病)是重要子主题,Wikidata + PMC 开放获取覆盖良好,建议单列条目。④建议为每条生理事实在档案库中双绑定 Wikidata QID + 一条公有领域史料/一篇 PMC 文献,形成『机器可查 ID + 可追溯出处』的双锚。

key_entities.name: Willo Welzenbach 分级(Welzenbach scale → UIAA scale)
key_entities.one_liner: 1923 年 Welzenbach 将 Benesch 1894 的七级制压缩并倒序为 I–VI,成为阿尔卑斯通用标准,1967 年正式定为 UIAA 分级。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1894 Fritz Benesch 首创(VII 最易);1923 Welzenbach 反转;1967 成 UIAA I–VI 闭合制;1978 加第七级;1985 开放上限至今 XII+。德/奥/瑞/捷/斯洛伐克仍用。

key_entities.name: Yosemite Decimal System (YDS)
key_entities.one_liner: 美国分级体系,1950s 由 Sierra Club(Robbins/Wilson/Wilts)将第五类细分为 5.0–5.9,后由 Bridwell 于 1970s 开放到 5.10 以上。
key_entities.notable_facts: 含 class 1–5 徒步到技术攀登、5.x 自由攀登难度、A0–A5 器械等级、罗马数字 I–VII 综合投入等级、R/X 保护风险后缀。现最高约 5.15d。

key_entities.name: 苏格兰冬季双分级(Scottish winter grade)
key_entities.one_liner: 1991 年确立的两级制:罗马数字表综合难度、阿拉伯数字表最难动作技术难度,专为传统保护的混合攀登设计。
key_entities.notable_facts: 如 'VI,8' 表示技术难度高于综合级;I–XI 开放;因用传统保护而对胆识/里程/体能加权,深刻影响现代 mixed/drytooling 语言。

key_entities.name: 法国分级(adjectival + numerical / Fontainebleau 源流)
key_entities.one_liner: 法国综合形容词级(F/PD/AD/D/TD/ED)评整条路线严肃度,数字自由攀级(源自枫丹白露抱石)评单段难度,是全球运动攀登主流体系。
key_entities.notable_facts: 形容词级用于阿尔卑斯路线综合评价;数字级 6a/7a/8a/9a 开放;与 UIAA/YDS 有标准换算表(UIAA 官方与 Wikidata 均有)。

key_entities.name: 冰镐 / piolet 演化
key_entities.one_liner: 由牧羊人 alpenstock(长杖)与砍冰小斧合并而来,Grivel 约 1840 造出首把真正冰镐,20 世纪逐步缩短、弯镐化。
key_entities.notable_facts: 19 世纪镐长 120–130cm 用于砍冰阶;Oscar Eckenstein 推短镐 85–86cm;1966 Yvon Chouinard 发明弯镐(curved pick)提升人体工学。

key_entities.name: 冰爪 / 前齿技术(crampons & front-pointing)
key_entities.one_liner: Oscar Eckenstein 20 世纪初设计 10 齿冰爪,Grivel 于 1932 加前伸两齿(front points),使前齿踢冰取代砍冰阶,革新冰雪技术。
key_entities.notable_facts: Laurent Grivel 1932 的 12 齿前齿冰爪是冰雪攀登效率跃升点;结合弯镐催生 1970s 'piolet traction' 双镐攀冰(Cecchinel/Jager 1973 Dru 北沟)。

key_entities.name: piolet traction 双镐攀冰技术
key_entities.one_liner: 1970s 确立的用两把带弯镐冰镐牵引攀陡冰的技术,1973 年 Walter Cecchinel 与 Claude Jager 首用于 Dru 北沟。
key_entities.notable_facts: 配合前齿冰爪把陡冰/瀑布冰从'砍阶数小时'变为可直接攀爬,直接催生现代 ice climbing 与 WI 分级。

key_entities.name: 岩钉 / piton 演化与 RURP
key_entities.one_liner: 从软铁一次性岩钉发展到 Chouinard 铬钼钢可复用岩钉,以及极小缝用 RURP(Realized Ultimate Reality Piton),是器械攀登黄金期的核心硬件。
key_entities.notable_facts: 铬钼钢岩钉可反复打入优质硬岩(Yosemite 花岗岩);反复打钉破坏岩体的问题正是 1972 clean climbing 革命的导火索。

key_entities.name: Chock / Nut(被动保护)与 clean climbing 革命
key_entities.one_liner: 1967 Robbins 从英国带回人工岩楔并用其首攀 Nutcracker;1972 Chouinard 装备目录倡导'clean climbing',以岩楔取代破坏岩体的岩钉。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1972 目录含 Chouinard-Frost 与 Doug Robinson《The Whole Natural Art of Protection》;英国传统的天然楔石/机械螺母演化为 Stopper/Hexcentric,确立'不留痕'伦理。

key_entities.name: Friends 弹簧凸轮装置(SLCD)
key_entities.one_liner: 航天工程师兼攀登者 Ray Jardine 发明的双组对置独立弹簧凸轮保护器,1977 由 Wild Country(Mark Vallance)量产,1978 首登广告。
key_entities.notable_facts: Jardine 1971 起研发,突破在于对置独立弹簧凸轮可在平行缝可靠受力;彻底改变平行缝/横缝的保护能力,是现代 trad 攀登基石。

key_entities.name: Kernmantle 动力绳与尼龙绳革命
key_entities.one_liner: 二战尼龙取代天然纤维绳,1953 年 Edelrid 推出芯鞘结构(kernmantle)动力绳,大幅提升缓冲坠落能力与耐用度。
key_entities.notable_facts: 从马特洪峰 1865 断绳事故的天然纤维静力绳,到尼龙 perlon 芯鞘动力绳,使'冲坠可保护'成为可能,是自由攀登能推极限的前提。

key_entities.name: 围攻式(siege / expedition style)
key_entities.one_liner: 19 世纪末军事化组织的登山法:固定绳、逐级建补给营、大量 porter/sherpa 与补充氧气,反复上下线路'围攻'高峰。
key_entities.notable_facts: 早期 8000 米首登(如 1953 珠峰、Annapurna)多为围攻式;Warren Harding 1958 首攀 El Cap 'The Nose' 用固定绳围攻式,是大墙版围攻。

key_entities.name: 阿式(alpine style)
key_entities.one_liner: 1970s 兴起的反主流风格:小队自背全部装备一次性冲顶,无 porter、无固定绳、无预设营地,强调速度、简约与承诺。
key_entities.notable_facts: Bonington 一代英国人与 Messner 为早期旗手;将阿尔卑斯轻快风格移植到喜马拉雅,重塑'纯粹性'伦理。

key_entities.name: Reinhold Messner
key_entities.one_liner: 阿式登山与无氧高海拔的化身:1978 与 Habeler 首次无氧登珠峰,1980 无氧单人阿式登珠峰,首位登完全部 14 座 8000 米。
key_entities.notable_facts: 将'by fair means'伦理推到极限;Himalayan Database 中其记录的 oxygen=None、style=alpine 字段是围攻→阿式转变的量化标志点。

key_entities.name: Yvon Chouinard
key_entities.one_liner: 攀登装备革新与伦理领袖:发明弯镐(1966)、铬钼钢岩钉,主导 1972 clean climbing 革命,后创 Patagonia。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1972 Chouinard Equipment 目录既是产品册也是环境宣言,一举把美国攀登从'打钉'转向'岩楔清洁攀登'。

key_entities.name: 自由攀登 vs 器械攀登(free vs aid climbing)
key_entities.one_liner: 自由攀登仅用手脚上升、器械只作坠落保护;器械攀登直接借助装备(钉/梯/凸轮)上升——二者的此消彼长定义了 20 世纪攀登伦理主线。
key_entities.notable_facts: 1960s–70s Yosemite 大墙是器械攀登黄金期(Robbins 'minimal aid' vs Harding);Robbins 极简伦理 + Chouinard clean 伦理推动自由化,终点如 1993 Lynn Hill 首次自由攀登 The Nose。

key_entities.name: Oscar Eckenstein
key_entities.one_liner: 英国攀登者与器械革新者,20 世纪初设计现代 10 齿冰爪并倡导短冰镐,奠定前齿冰雪技术的硬件基础。
key_entities.notable_facts: 其冰爪设计经 Grivel 1932 加前齿后成现代冰爪;短镐理念终结了长 alpenstock 时代。

notes:
范围与方法:本轮聚焦 gear-technique 子领域四条主线(装备演化、分级体系、围攻 vs 阿式、自由 vs 器械),经 7 组网络检索交叉印证,事实点均有 2+ 来源支撑(UIAA/Wikipedia 分级谱系、Grivel/AAC 冰镐史、Wild Country/Jardine 凸轮史、Himalayan Database 风格字段)。

许可分层(建档复用优先级):
1) 可无条件整批复用:**Wikidata(CC0)**——建议作结构化骨架,SPARQL 拉分级体系/装备/人物 item;**OSM climbing + OpenBeta(ODbL/部分 CC0)**——唯一可复用的路线级多体系分级数据。
2) 署名即可复用:**Wikipedia(CC-BY-SA)**正文底稿;**Wikisource/Gutenberg(公版)**老文本可全文入库(Whymper #41234/#38044、Mummery)。注意:公版仅限文本本身,当代重排版/扫描图需逐件核。
3) 仅提取事实、不可复制表达/图:**AAC/AAJ 全文库**、**UIAA 官方文档**、**Himalayan Database**——'谁在何时以何风格首登、用何保护、路线分级'这类事实不受版权保护可入档,但其撰写文字与照片受版权,不可直接再发布。

版权红线提示:近代(约 1929 年后)攀登照片、当代 AAJ/杂志文字、装备厂商官网叙述(Grivel/Wild Country blog)属受版权表达,仅可作事实核验与引注,不可整段/整图搬入档案库;建档时对每条实体标注'事实来源'与'表达/图片是否可复用'两栏。

尚未覆盖(建议后续轮):冰瀑 WI/M 混合分级细则、萨克森(Saxon/Elbsandstein)与英国传统 E-grade 谱系、法国 Fontainebleau 抱石级与 V-scale/Font-scale 换算、UIAA 装备安全标准(EN/UIAA 认证)演化、绳索 UIAA fall rating 技术史。
